tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp — reproduction note
===========================================================

This file is a hand-crafted Ed25519-primary OpenPGP public key, generated once,
bytes committed. We do NOT regenerate at CI time because Ed25519 key generation
uses OS randomness — same recipe produces a different fixture each run (Name/
Email/Expire deterministic; key bytes + signature bytes random).

Deterministic content:
  Primary algorithm:  Ed25519 (RFC 4880 §13.2 / RFC 9580 §5.5.5.5; algo ID 22 EDDSA)
  Primary UID:        cp <f@cp.t>             (10 chars — under D-P7-03 + GAP 5 ≤20-char budget)
  Subkeys:            none
  Expiration:         none (Expire-Date: 0)
  Packet version:     v4 (40-hex SHA-1 fingerprint; RFC 4880)

Generation recipe (requires GnuPG 2.2+):

  export GNUPGHOME=$(mktemp -d)
  cat > /tmp/batch <<EOF
  %no-protection
  %no-ask-passphrase
  Key-Type: EDDSA
  Key-Curve: ed25519
  Name-Real: cp
  Name-Email: f@cp.t
  Expire-Date: 0
  %commit
  EOF
  gpg --batch --generate-key /tmp/batch
  KEYID=$(gpg --list-keys --with-colons | awk -F: '/^pub/ {print $5; exit}')
  gpg --export "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp
  gpg --export-secret-keys "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp

Verification (deterministic on parser state, not signature bytes):

  gpg --list-packets < tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp
  # ->  :public key packet: algo 22 (EDDSA), version 4
  #     :user ID packet: "cp <f@cp.t>"
  #     :signature packet: algo 22 (EDDSA), digest algo 10 (SHA-512), sigclass 0x13

Fixture size budget (Phase 7 research GAP 5 + D-P7-03):
  raw packet stream:     215 bytes (target ≤200 B; allow ≤230 B per acceptance band)
  predicted encoded:     215 × 4.16 ≈ 894 bytes (fits under 1000 B PKARR BEP44)
  Actual public size:    215 bytes
  Actual secret size:    252 bytes

SHA-256 of the committed file (recorded at generation time, for tamper
detection during reviews):
  public:  f5e5a79c6570f8531f39d1cdbae772d09ed7cbfa0eeb664c644788001626daa8
  secret:  86e4fe6e5cf7c354439050e676178c48f8858b5501124f321e69991859a8f1ca

(The SHA-256 above will MISMATCH on regeneration because Ed25519 key bytes are
random — the values are recorded for forensic tamper detection across reviewers
working with the SAME committed bytes, not as a regeneration target.)

PGP fingerprint as rendered by `gpg --list-keys --with-fingerprint` for the
committed copy:
  8F718A07242DC77D95FC8A5A45A2450314668B99   (40-hex SHA-1, v4 format)

Secret-key fixture: tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp
  Generated via: gpg --export-secret-keys "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp
  Purpose: golden-string test for [WARNING: SECRET key] line placement
           (preview::render_pgp_preview tag-5 path; D-P7-07).

JCS envelope fixture: tests/fixtures/material_pgp_signable.bin
  Regenerate via:
    cargo test --test material_pgp_envelope_round_trip -- --ignored regenerate_material_pgp_envelope_fixture
  Then commit the new bytes; the byte-identity assertion will lock the new shape.
